fixes.origin:
with open(filename, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    command = f.read().strip()

proc = subprocess.run(command, shell=True, capture_output=True, encoding="utf-8")
fixes.replacement:
with open(filename, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    command = f.read().strip().lower()
allowed = {"whoami": ["whoami"], "date": ["date"], "id": ["id"]}
base = allowed.get(command)
if base is None:
    raise ValueError("Unsupported command in file")
proc = subprocess.run(base, shell=False, capture_output=True, encoding="utf-8")

static_findings.message: This file operation appears to use a user-controlled path without proper validation, which may allow directory traversal or access to unintended files.
subprocess execution may allow OS command injection if input is not controlled.
